revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shidami otsuka kofun.jpg|300px]] |Caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}} Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{coord|35|15|10.34|N|137|02|28.66|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |Size = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |Era = Late 5th century |Historic Designation = National Historic Site (part of {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |Artifacts = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |Map = Japan Aichi |Icon = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |Label = |Label Position = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient burial mounds scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Tumulus," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== Structure and Dimensions == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a short anterior portion. Its dimensions are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== Burial Facilities ==
=== Primary Burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary Burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== Artifacts == Artifacts from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun."
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shidami otsuka kofun.jpg|300px]] |Caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}} Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{coord|35|15|10.34|N|137|02|28.66|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |Size = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |Era = Late 5th century |Historic Designation = National Historic Site (part of {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |Artifacts = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |Map = Japan Aichi |Icon = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |Label = |Label Position = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient burial mounds scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Tumulus," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== Structure and Dimensions == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a short anterior portion. Its dimensions are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== Burial Facilities ==
=== Primary Burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary Burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== Artifacts == Artifacts from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun."
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shidami otsuka kofun.jpg|300px]] |Caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}} Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{coord|35|15|10.34|N|137|02|28.66|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |Size = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |Era = Late 5th century |Historic Designation = National Historic Site (part of {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |Artifacts = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |Map = Japan Aichi |Icon = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |Label = |Label Position = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient burial mounds scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Tumulus," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== Structure and Dimensions == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a short anterior portion. Its dimensions are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== Burial Facilities ==
=== Primary Burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary Burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== Artifacts == Artifacts from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun."
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shidami otsuka kofun.jpg|300px]] |Caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}} Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{coord|35|15|10.34|N|137|02|28.66|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |Size = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |Era = Late 5th century |Historic Designation = National Historic Site (part of {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |Artifacts = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |Map = Japan Aichi |Icon = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |Label = |Label Position = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient burial mounds scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Tumulus," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== Structure and Dimensions == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a short anterior portion. Its dimensions are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== Burial Facilities ==
=== Primary Burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary Burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== Artifacts == Artifacts from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun."
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shidami otsuka kofun.jpg|300px]] |Caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}} Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{coord|35|15|10.34|N|137|02|28.66|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |Size = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |Era = Late 5th century |Historic Designation = National Historic Site (part of {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |Artifacts = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |Map = Japan Aichi |Icon = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |Label = |Label Position = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient burial mounds scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Tumulus," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== Structure and Dimensions == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a short anterior portion. Its dimensions are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== Burial Facilities ==
=== Primary Burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary Burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== Artifacts == Artifacts from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun."
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shidami otsuka kofun.jpg|300px]] |Caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}} Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{coord|35|15|10.34|N|137|02|28.66|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |Size = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |Era = Late 5th century |Historic Designation = National Historic Site (part of {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |Artifacts = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |Map = Japan Aichi |Icon = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |Label = |Label Position = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient burial mounds scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Tumulus," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== Structure and Dimensions == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a short anterior portion. Its dimensions are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== Burial Facilities ==
=== Primary Burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary Burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== Artifacts == Artifacts from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun."
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "burial mound" to "Kofun"
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shidami otsuka kofun.jpg|300px]] |Caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}} Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{coord|35|15|10.34|N|137|02|28.66|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |Size = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |Era = Late 5th century |Historic Designation = National Historic Site (part of {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |Artifacts = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |Map = Japan Aichi |Icon = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |Label = |Label Position = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Tumulus," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== Structure and Dimensions == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a short anterior portion. Its dimensions are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== Burial Facilities ==
=== Primary Burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary Burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== Artifacts == Artifacts from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun."
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== Structure and Dimensions == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a short anterior portion. Its dimensions are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== Burial Facilities ==
=== Primary Burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary Burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== Artifacts == Artifacts from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun."
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shidami otsuka kofun.jpg|300px]] |Caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}} Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{coord|35|15|10.34|N|137|02|28.66|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |Size = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |Era = Late 5th century |Historic Designation = National Historic Site (part of {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |Artifacts = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |Map = Japan Aichi |Icon = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |Label = |Label Position = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== Structure and Dimensions == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a short anterior portion. Its dimensions are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== Burial Facilities ==
=== Primary Burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary Burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== Artifacts == Artifacts from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun."
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shidami otsuka kofun.jpg|300px]] |Caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}} Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{coord|35|15|10.34|N|137|02|28.66|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |Size = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |Era = Late 5th century |Historic Designation = National Historic Site (part of {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |Artifacts = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |Map = Japan Aichi |Icon = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |Label = |Label Position = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== Structure and Dimensions == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a short anterior portion. Its dimensions are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== Burial Facilities ==
=== Primary Burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary Burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== Artifacts == Artifacts from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun."
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shidami otsuka kofun.jpg|300px]] |Caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}} Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{coord|35|15|10.34|N|137|02|28.66|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |Size = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |Era = Late 5th century |Historic Designation = National Historic Site (part of {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |Artifacts = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |Map = Japan Aichi |Icon = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |Label = |Label Position = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== Structure and Dimensions == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a short anterior portion. Its dimensions are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== Burial Facilities ==
=== Primary Burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary Burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== Artifacts == Artifacts from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun."
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shidami otsuka kofun.jpg|300px]] |Caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}} Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{coord|35|15|10.34|N|137|02|28.66|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |Size = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |Era = Late 5th century |Historic Designation = National Historic Site (part of {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |Artifacts = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |Map = Japan Aichi |Icon = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |Label = |Label Position = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== Structure and Dimensions == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a short anterior portion. Its dimensions are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== Burial Facilities ==
=== Primary Burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary Burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== Artifacts == Artifacts from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun."
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shidami otsuka kofun.jpg|300px]] |Caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}} Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{coord|35|15|10.34|N|137|02|28.66|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |Size = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |Era = Late 5th century |Historic Designation = National Historic Site (part of {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |Artifacts = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |Map = Japan Aichi |Icon = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |Label = |Label Position = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== Structure and Dimensions == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a short anterior portion. Its dimensions are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== Burial Facilities ==
=== Primary Burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary Burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== Artifacts == Artifacts from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun."
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shidami otsuka kofun.jpg|300px]] |Caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}} Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{coord|35|15|10.34|N|137|02|28.66|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |Size = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |Era = Late 5th century |Historic Designation = National Historic Site (part of {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |Artifacts = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |Map = Japan Aichi |Icon = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |Label = |Label Position = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== Structure and Dimensions == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a short anterior portion. Its dimensions are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== Burial Facilities ==
=== Primary Burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary Burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== Artifacts == Artifacts from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun."== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

== Structure and Dimensions == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a short anterior portion. Its dimensions are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== Burial Facilities ==
=== Primary Burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary Burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== Artifacts == Artifacts from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shidami otsuka kofun.jpg|300px]] |Caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}} Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{coord|35|15|10.34|N|137|02|28.66|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |Size = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |Era = Late 5th century |Historic Designation = National Historic Site (part of {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |Map = Japan Aichi |Icon = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |Label = |Label Position = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== Structure and Dimensions == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a short anterior portion. Its dimensions are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== Burial Facilities ==
=== Primary Burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary Burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== Artifacts == Artifacts from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shidami otsuka kofun.jpg|300px]] |Caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}} Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{coord|35|15|10.34|N|137|02|28.66|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |Size = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |Era = Late 5th century |Historic Designation = National Historic Site (part of {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |Map = Japan Aichi |Icon = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |Label = |Label Position = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== Structure and Dimensions == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a short anterior portion. Its dimensions are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== Burial Facilities ==
=== Primary Burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary Burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== Artifacts == Artifacts from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shidami otsuka kofun.jpg|300px]] |Caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}} Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{coord|35|15|10.34|N|137|02|28.66|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |Size = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |Era = Late 5th century |Historic Designation = National Historic Site (part of {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |map = Japan Aichi |Icon = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label Position = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== Structure and Dimensions == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a short anterior portion. Its dimensions are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== Burial Facilities ==
=== Primary Burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary Burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== Artifacts == Artifacts from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.

=== Primary Burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary Burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== Artifacts == Artifacts from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== Structure and area == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a short anterior portion. Its dimensions are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== Burial Facilities ==
=== Primary Burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary Burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== Artifacts == Artifacts from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]
