This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shidami otsuka kofun.jpg|300px]] |Caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}} Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{coord|35|15|10.34|N|137|02|28.66|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |area = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |Era = Late 5th century |alternate_name = National other_designation (part of {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |map = Japan Aichi |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}, other_designation1 as a National other_designation.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== Structure and area == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a short anterior portion. Its area are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== burial ==
=== Primary burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== burial goods == burial goods from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shidami otsuka kofun.jpg|300px]] |caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}} Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{coord|35|15|10.34|N|137|02|28.66|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |area = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |built = Late 5th century |alternate_name = National other_designation (part of {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |map = Japan Aichi |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}, other_designation1 as a National other_designation.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== burial and area == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a short anterior portion. Its area are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== burial ==
=== Primary burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== burial goods == burial goods from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shidami otsuka kofun.jpg|300px]] |caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}} Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{coord|35|15|10.34|N|137|02|28.66|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |area = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |built = Late 5th century |alternate_name = National other_designation (part of {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |map = Japan Aichi |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}, other_designation1 as a National other_designation.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== burial and area == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a short anterior portion. Its area are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== burial ==
=== Primary burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== burial goods == burial goods from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shidami otsuka kofun.jpg|300px]] |caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}} Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{coord|35|15|10.34|N|137|02|28.66|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |area = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |built = Late 5th century |alternate_name = National Historic Site (part of {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |map = Japan Aichi |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}, Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== burial and area == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a short anterior portion. Its area are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== burial ==
=== Primary burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== burial goods == burial goods from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shidami otsuka kofun.jpg|300px]] |caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}} Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{coord|35|15|10.34|N|137|02|28.66|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |area = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |built = Late 5th century |alternate_name = National Historic Site (part of {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |map = Japan Aichi |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}, Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== burial and area == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a short anterior portion. Its area are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== burial ==
=== Primary burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== burial goods == burial goods from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shidami otsuka kofun.jpg|300px]] |caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}} Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{coord|35|15|10.34|N|137|02|28.66|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |area = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |built = Late 5th century |alternate_name = National Historic Site (part of {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |map = Japan Aichi |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}, Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== burial and area == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a short anterior portion. Its area are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== burial ==
=== Primary burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== burial goods == burial goods from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=draft}} |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shidami otsuka kofun.jpg|300px]] |caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|12=draft}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区|12=draft}} Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{coord|35|15|10.34|N|137|02|28.66|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |area = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |built = Late 5th century |alternate_name = National Historic Site (part of {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群|12=draft}}) |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |map = Japan Aichi |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群|12=draft}}, Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=draft}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== burial and area == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} with a short anterior portion. Its area are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== burial ==
=== Primary burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== burial goods == burial goods from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅|12=draft}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線|12=draft}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅|12=draft}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

== burial goods == burial goods from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅|12=draft}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線|12=draft}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅|12=draft}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=draft}} |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shidami otsuka kofun.jpg|300px]] |caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|12=draft}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区|12=draft}} Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{coord|35|15|10.34|N|137|02|28.66|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |area = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |built = Late 5th century |alternate_name = National Historic Site (part of {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群|12=draft}}) |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |map = Japan Aichi |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群|12=draft}}, Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=draft}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== burial and area == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} with a short anterior portion. Its area are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== burial ==
=== Primary burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== burial goods == burial goods from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅|12=draft}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線|12=draft}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅|12=draft}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=draft}} |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shidami otsuka kofun.jpg|300px]] |caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|12=draft}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区|12=draft}} Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{coord|35|15|10.34|N|137|02|28.66|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |area = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |built = Late 5th century |alternate_name = National Historic Site (part of {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群|12=draft}}) |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |map = Japan Aichi |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群|12=draft}}, Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=draft}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== burial and area == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} with a short anterior portion. Its area are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== burial ==
=== Primary burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== burial goods == burial goods from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅|12=draft}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線|12=draft}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅|12=draft}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅|12=draft}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線|12=draft}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅|12=draft}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}}
== References == {{reflist}}

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=draft}} |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shidami otsuka kofun.jpg|300px]] |caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|12=draft}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区|12=draft}} Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{coord|35|15|10.34|N|137|02|28.66|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |area = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |built = Late 5th century |alternate_name = National Historic Site (part of {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群|12=draft}}) |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |map = Japan Aichi |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群|12=draft}}, Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=draft}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== burial and area == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} with a short anterior portion. Its area are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== burial ==
=== Primary burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== burial goods == burial goods from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅|12=draft}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線|12=draft}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅|12=draft}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=draft}} |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shidami otsuka kofun.jpg|300px]] |caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|12=draft}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区|12=draft}} Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{coord|35|15|10.34|N|137|02|28.66|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |area = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |built = Late 5th century |alternate_name = National Historic Site (part of {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群|12=draft}}) |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |map = Japan Aichi |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群|12=draft}}, Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=draft}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== burial and area == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} with a short anterior portion. Its area are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== burial ==
=== Primary burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== burial goods == burial goods from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅|12=draft}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線|12=draft}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅|12=draft}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

{{日本の古墳 |画像 = [[画像:Shidami otsuka kofun.jpg|300px]] |画像キャプション = 志段味大塚古墳 |名称 = 志段味大塚古墳 |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[守山区]]上志段味字大塚 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 15|緯度秒 = 10.34 |経度度 = 137|経度分 = 02|経度秒 = 28.66 |形状 = 帆立貝形前方後円墳 |規模 = 墳丘長 約55m、全長 約62m（周濠含む）、後円部径約40m、後円部高 約7m、前方部高 約1.5m |築造年代 = [[5世紀]]後半 |史跡指定 = 国の[[史跡]]「志段味大塚古墳」
（「[[志段味古墳群]]」に包含） |出土品 = 銅鏡、馬具、刀剣類など |地図 = Japan Aichi |地図2 = |アイコン = 帆立貝形古墳-南西 |ラベル = |ラベル位置 = bottom }} [[ファイル:Shidami Otsuka kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|2008年当時の志段味大塚古墳（[[2008年]]（[[平成]]20年）7月）]] '''志段味大塚古墳'''（しだみおおつかこふん）は[[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[守山区]]にある[[古墳]]。[[志段味古墳群]]のひとつとして国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。 == 概要 == 大久手池の周辺に点在する古墳群の中で最大規模の[[前方後円墳]]。周辺が大塚遺跡と呼ばれたこともあり、その中の'''大塚1号墳'''とも呼称される。 [[1923年]]（[[大正]]12年）に[[京都大学|京都帝国大学]]の[[梅原末治]]によって行われた後円部の発掘調査では、墳頂部から[[粘土槨]]の主体部（埋葬施設）が発見され、内部から多くの副葬品が出土した。しかし古墳自体はその後長らく放置され、[[1982年]]（[[昭和]]57年）・[[1983年]]（昭和58年）になってようやく[[愛知県教育委員会]]の依頼で[[名古屋大学]]による測量調査が行われた。[[2005年]]（[[平成]]17年）以降、[[名古屋市教育委員会]]による調査が行われているが、年月を経る間に外観はかなり失われている。なお、[[2008年]]（平成20年）の調査時に梅原が[[発掘調査]]をおこなったものとは別の主体部を確認している（後述）。 == 規模と構造 == 前方部が短い[[帆立貝形古墳]]で、墳丘は全長約55メートル（周濠部分を含めると全長約62メートル）、後円部の直径が約40メートル（主軸方向に1メートルほど長い[[楕円形]]）、墳高は後円部で約7メートル、前方部で約1.5メートルと推定される、主軸を北東方向（わずかに北寄り）に向けている。墳丘には[[円筒埴輪]]が置かれ、墳裾部に河原石の[[葺石]]を巡らしていた。また、後円部にはテラス面が確認されているほか、後円部から見た右側くびれ部に[[造出]]が付設されており（ただし大部分は[[江戸時代]]以降に削られたと見られる）、その上部に埴輪列の一部が残存している。 前方部の傾斜角と葺石の施工が変化している構造を持っており、周濠側では約10度と緩やかで拳大の円礫を敷き詰めるように葺いているが、墳丘側では約40度の傾斜を持つ部分もあり、大き目で平たい河原石を、5センチメートルから10センチメートルの隙間をあけて平面を外に向けて貼り付けたように葺いている。前方部の傾斜角が変化する同様の構造は、周辺では[[西大久手古墳]]のみとされる。 周濠は一重で馬蹄形に墳丘を囲んでいると推定され、緩やかな逆台形もしくはV字形の断面を持つ。外堤上部に幅0.5メートルほどの平坦面が確認されるなどしているが、開発により一部は舗装道路下に埋没している。 == 主体部 == 前述の通り、1923年（大正12年）の梅原による発掘調査以降は目を向けられることのなかった本古墳だが、2008年（平成20年）の発掘調査で北東から南西方向（墳丘主軸とほぼ平行）に主軸を向けた「もう1つの主体部」残存が確認された。新規主体部の規模は墓壙が長さ5.8メートル、幅3メートルで、前方部側に幅0.9メートルの通路状遺構（長さ不明）があった。[[木棺]]そのものは確認できなかったが土層から赤色[[顔料]]が見つかるなどしており、内面に[[朱]]を塗った長さ3.6 - 3.9メートル、幅約1メートルの刳抜式木棺が直接埋葬されていた（木棺直葬墓）とも推定されている。梅原主体部の記録では埋葬した際の頭位は大刀の副葬状況から北東だったと思われ、新規主体部についても梅原主体部と並列し頭位も同様だったと考えられているが、両者の埋葬された時期的な関係などは不明である。 なお、木棺があったとされる場所の土層から[[漆]]膜が見つかっており、木棺上に置かれていた漆塗[[盾|皮盾]]が、木棺が朽ちた後に落下した痕跡と考えられるという。 == 出土品 == ; [[京都大学総合博物館]]所蔵 * [[銅鏡|五鈴鏡]] * 金銅製帯金具 * [[挂甲]]小札 * [[鉄鏃]] * 木芯鉄板張輪鐙 * 鋳銅五鈴杏葉 * 五鈴鏡板付轡 * 三環鈴 * [[刀剣]]類 ; [[名古屋市博物館]]所蔵 * 挂甲小札 * [[埴輪]]片 ; [[見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|見晴台考古資料館]]所蔵 * 水鳥形埴輪 * 鶏形埴輪 * 蓋形埴輪 * 朝顔形埴輪 * 円筒埴輪 * 坏 * はそう（瓦泉） * 無蓋高坏 * 有蓋高坏 * 挂甲小札片 * [[鉄器]] == 参考文献 == * [[名古屋市教育委員会]] 『志段味大塚古墳・大久手古墳群 - 平成17年度大久手池周辺埋蔵文化財発掘調査報告書』、[[2006年]] * 名古屋市教育委員会 『大久手池周辺古墳群 - 平成18年度「歴史の里（仮称）」計画地内埋蔵文化財範囲確認調査』、[[2007年]] * 新修名古屋市史史料編編集委員会 『[[新修名古屋市史]] 資料編 考古1』名古屋市、[[2008年]] * 名古屋市教育委員会・国際航業株式会社 『志段味大塚古墳現地説明会資料』、2008年 * [[名古屋市見晴台考古資料館]] 『名古屋市見晴台考古資料館報 みはらし No.257』、2008年 * 名古屋市教育委員会 『志段味古墳群 -平成19・20年度「歴史の里」計画地内埋蔵文化財調査の概要-』、[[2009年]] * 名古屋市見晴台考古資料館 『名古屋市見晴台考古資料館報 みはらし No.261』、2009年 * 藤井康隆編[http://www.book61.co.jp/book.php/N06852 『尾張氏☆志段味古墳群をときあかす』][http://www.museum.city.nagoya.jp/ 名古屋市博物館]、2012年 == アクセス == * [[大曽根駅]]（[[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[中央線 (名古屋地区)|中央本線]]、[[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄瀬戸線|瀬戸線]]、[[名古屋市営地下鉄]][[名古屋市営地下鉄名城線|名城線]]）より、 ** [[名古屋ガイドウェイバス]][[名古屋ガイドウェイバスガイドウェイバス志段味線|志段味線]]（[[ゆとりーとライン]]）「新東谷橋南」停留所下車。 * [[高蔵寺駅]]（JR中央本線、[[愛知環状鉄道]]・[[愛知環状鉄道線]]）より、 ** [[名古屋市営バス]]：志段味巡回系統「志段味大塚古墳」停留所下車。 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Shidami Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 中国と六朝の扉・大久手古墳群発掘調査日記] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:したみおおつかこふん}} [[Category:名古屋市の古墳]] [[Category:守山区の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:愛知県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:志段味古墳群]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[志段味大塚古墳]] to [[Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

[[京都大学総合博物館]]所蔵 * [[銅鏡|五鈴鏡]] * 金銅製帯金具 * [[挂甲]]小札 * [[鉄鏃]] * 木芯鉄板張輪鐙 * 鋳銅五鈴杏葉 * 五鈴鏡板付轡 * 三環鈴 * [[刀剣]]類 ; [[名古屋市博物館]]所蔵 * 挂甲小札 * [[埴輪]]片 ; [[見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|見晴台考古資料館]]所蔵 * 水鳥形埴輪 * 鶏形埴輪 * 蓋形埴輪 * 朝顔形埴輪 * 円筒埴輪 * 坏 * はそう（瓦泉） * 無蓋高坏 * 有蓋高坏 * 挂甲小札片 * [[鉄器]] == 参考文献 == * [[名古屋市教育委員会]] 『志段味大塚古墳・大久手古墳群 - 平成17年度大久手池周辺埋蔵文化財発掘調査報告書』、[[2006年]] * 名古屋市教育委員会 『大久手池周辺古墳群 - 平成18年度「歴史の里（仮称）」計画地内埋蔵文化財範囲確認調査』、[[2007年]] * 新修名古屋市史史料編編集委員会 『[[新修名古屋市史]] 資料編 考古1』名古屋市、[[2008年]] * 名古屋市教育委員会・国際航業株式会社 『志段味大塚古墳現地説明会資料』、2008年 * [[名古屋市見晴台考古資料館]] 『名古屋市見晴台考古資料館報 みはらし No.257』、2008年 * 名古屋市教育委員会 『志段味古墳群 -平成19・20年度「歴史の里」計画地内埋蔵文化財調査の概要-』、[[2009年]] * 名古屋市見晴台考古資料館 『名古屋市見晴台考古資料館報 みはらし No.261』、2009年 * 藤井康隆編[http://www.book61.co.jp/book.php/N06852 『尾張氏☆志段味古墳群をときあかす』][http://www.museum.city.nagoya.jp/ 名古屋市博物館]、2012年 == アクセス == * [[大曽根駅]]（[[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[中央線 (名古屋地区)|中央本線]]、[[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄瀬戸線|瀬戸線]]、[[名古屋市営地下鉄]][[名古屋市営地下鉄名城線|名城線]]）より、 ** [[名古屋ガイドウェイバス]][[名古屋ガイドウェイバスガイドウェイバス志段味線|志段味線]]（[[ゆとりーとライン]]）「新東谷橋南」停留所下車。 * [[高蔵寺駅]]（JR中央本線、[[愛知環状鉄道]]・[[愛知環状鉄道線]]）より、 ** [[名古屋市営バス]]：志段味巡回系統「志段味大塚古墳」停留所下車。 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Shidami Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 中国と六朝の扉・大久手古墳群発掘調査日記] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:したみおおつかこふん}} [[Category:名古屋市の古墳]] [[Category:守山区の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:愛知県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:志段味古墳群]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shidami Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:志段味大塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11491065]] Wikidata name: Shidami Otsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = File:Shidami Otsuka kofun.jpg |caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]] [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{Coord|35.252872|N|137.041294|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |area = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |built = Late 5th century |alternate_name = National Historic Site (part of [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun Group]]) |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |map = Japan Aichi |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun Group]], Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== burial and area == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a short anterior portion. Its area are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== burial ==
=== Primary burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== burial goods == burial goods from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From [[:en:Ōzone Station|Ōzone Station]] (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From [[:en:Kōzōji Station|Kōzōji Station]] (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== burial and area == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a short anterior portion. Its area are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== burial ==
=== Primary burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== burial goods == burial goods from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From [[:en:Ōzone Station|Ōzone Station]] (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From [[:en:Kōzōji Station|Kōzōji Station]] (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== burial and area == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a short anterior portion. Its area are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== burial ==
=== Primary burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== burial goods == burial goods from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shidami Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:志段味大塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11491065]] Wikidata name: Shidami Otsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = File:Shidami Otsuka kofun.jpg |caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]] [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{Coord|35.252872|N|137.041294|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |area = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |built = Late 5th century |alternate_name = National Historic Site (part of [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun Group]]) |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |map = Japan Aichi |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun Group]], Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== burial and area == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a short anterior portion. Its area are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== burial ==
=== Primary burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== burial goods == burial goods from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From [[:en:Ōzone Station|Ōzone Station]] (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From [[:en:Kōzōji Station|Kōzōji Station]] (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== burial ==
=== Primary burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== burial goods == burial goods from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From [[:en:Ōzone Station|Ōzone Station]] (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From [[:en:Kōzōji Station|Kōzōji Station]] (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
