'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun Group]], Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== burial and area == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a short anterior portion. Its area are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== burial ==
=== Primary burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== burial goods == burial goods from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From [[:en:Ōzone Station|Ōzone Station]] (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From [[:en:Kōzōji Station|Kōzōji Station]] (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = File:Shidami Otsuka kofun.jpg |caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]] [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{Coord|35.252872|N|137.041294|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |area = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |built = Late 5th century |alternate_name = National Historic Site (part of [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun Group]]) |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |map = Japan Aichi |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun Group]], Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== burial and area == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a short anterior portion. Its area are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== burial ==
=== Primary burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== burial goods == burial goods from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From [[:en:Ōzone Station|Ōzone Station]] (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From [[:en:Kōzōji Station|Kōzōji Station]] (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== burial and area == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a short anterior portion. Its area are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== burial ==
=== Primary burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== burial goods == burial goods from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From [[:en:Ōzone Station|Ōzone Station]] (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From [[:en:Kōzōji Station|Kōzōji Station]] (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun Group]], Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== burial and area == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a short anterior portion. Its area are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== burial ==
=== Primary burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== burial goods == burial goods from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From [[:en:Ōzone Station|Ōzone Station]] (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From [[:en:Kōzōji Station|Kōzōji Station]] (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shidami Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:志段味大塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shidami Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:志段味大塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shidami Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:志段味大塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11491065]] Wikidata name: Shidami Otsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = File:Shidami Otsuka kofun.jpg |caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]] [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{Coord|35.252872|N|137.041294|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |area = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |built = Late 5th century |alternate_name = National Historic Site (part of [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun Group]]) |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |map = Japan Aichi |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun Group]], Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== burial and area == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a short anterior portion. Its area are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== burial ==
=== Primary burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== burial goods == burial goods from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From [[:en:Ōzone Station|Ōzone Station]] (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From [[:en:Kōzōji Station|Kōzōji Station]] (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = File:Shidami Otsuka kofun.jpg |caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}} {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya}} Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{Coord|35.252872|N|137.041294|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |area = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |built = Late 5th century |alternate_name = National Historic Site (part of {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|en|Shidami Kofun group|WD=Q11491064|lt=Shidami Kofun Group}}) |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |map = Japan Aichi |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|en|Shidami Kofun group|WD=Q11491064|lt=Shidami Kofun Group}}, Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== burial and area == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a short anterior portion. Its area are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== burial ==
=== Primary burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== burial goods == burial goods from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|en|Ōzone Station|WD=Q872075|lt=Ōzone Station}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|en|Kōzōji Station|WD=Q5359256|lt=Kōzōji Station}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shidami Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:志段味大塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shidami Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:志段味大塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Shidami Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:志段味大塚古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |image = File:Shidami Otsuka kofun.jpg |caption = Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}} {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|1=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya}} Kamishidami Ōtsuka |coordinates = {{Coord|35.252872|N|137.041294|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Hottate-gata Zenpō-kōen-fun) |area = Mound length approx. 55m; Total length (including moat) approx. 62m; Rear circular diameter approx. 40m; Rear circular height approx. 7m; Front height approx. 1.5m |built = Late 5th century |alternate_name = National Historic Site (part of {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|en|Shidami Kofun group|WD=Q11491064|lt=Shidami Kofun Group|1=Shidami Kofun group}}) |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, horse fittings, swords, and more |map = Japan Aichi |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SW |map_dot_label = |map_dot_label = Bottom }}
'''Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun''' (志段味大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|1=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|en|Shidami Kofun group|WD=Q11491064|lt=Shidami Kofun Group|1=Shidami Kofun group}}, Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site.
== Overview == Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun is the largest {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) within the ancient Kofuns scattered around the Ōgute Pond area. Historically referred to as "Ōtsuka No. 1 Kofun," it has undergone several phases of investigation and conservation.
The tumulus was first excavated in 1923 by Suematsu Umehara of Kyoto Imperial University, revealing a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} burial facility and numerous grave goods. However, the site remained largely neglected until surveys by Nagoya University in 1982-83 and further research by Nagoya City Board of Education starting in 2005. Despite these efforts, significant degradation occurred due to the passage of time.
A significant discovery in 2008 revealed a second burial facility parallel to the mound's axis, distinct from the earlier documented structure.
== burial and area == The tumulus is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a short anterior portion. Its area are as follows: * Total length of the mound: approx. 55m (approx. 62m including moat) * Diameter of the rear circular portion: approx. 40m * Height of the rear circular portion: approx. 7m * Height of the anterior portion: approx. 1.5m
The mound features terraced levels on the rear circular section, a surrounding stone-paved base, and a partial haniwa arrangement. A moat encircles the tumulus in a horseshoe configuration.
== burial ==
=== Primary burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== burial goods == burial goods from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|en|Ōzone Station|WD=Q872075|lt=Ōzone Station|1=Ōzone Station}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|en|Kōzōji Station|WD=Q5359256|lt=Kōzōji Station|1=Kōzōji Station}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/archive/c163489-1 Blog on the Shidami Kofun Group Excavations]

=== Primary burial === The primary burial discovered in 1923 contained grave goods including bronze mirrors and weapons. The burial orientation is believed to face northeast based on the arrangement of grave goods.
=== Secondary burial === The secondary burial unearthed in 2008 featured a mortuary pit measuring 5.8m in length and 3m in width, with an adjacent passage-like structure. It likely housed a carved wooden coffin painted with vermilion pigment.
== burial goods == burial goods from the tumulus are housed in various institutions: * '''Kyoto University Museum''': ** Bronze mirrors ** Gilded belt fittings ** Iron arrowheads ** Horse fittings ** Weapons * '''Nagoya City Museum''': ** Armor fragments ** Haniwa fragments * '''Miharashidai Archaeological Museum''': ** Waterfowl-shaped haniwa ** Chicken-shaped haniwa ** Cylindrical haniwa ** Pottery fragments
== Access == * From {{ill|Ōzone Station|en|Ōzone Station|WD=Q872075|lt=Ōzone Station|1=Ōzone Station}} (JR Chūō Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line): ** Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Yutorīto Line) to "Shintōyabashi-Minami." * From {{ill|Kōzōji Station|en|Kōzōji Station|WD=Q5359256|lt=Kōzōji Station|1=Kōzōji Station}} (JR Chūō Line, Aichi Loop Line): ** Take the Nagoya City Bus, Shidami Circulation Route, to "Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun." == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
